chapters.zh: 奉我们救主神和我们的盼望基督耶稣之命，作基督耶稣使徒的保罗，
chapters.en: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the commandment of God our Saviour, and Lord Jesus Christ, our hope;

chapters.zh: 写信给那因信主作我真儿子的提摩太。愿恩惠、怜悯、平安从父神和我们主基督耶稣归与你！
chapters.en: Unto Timothy, own son in the faith: Grace, mercy, peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.

chapters.zh: 我往马其顿去的时候，曾劝你仍住在以弗所，好嘱咐那几个人不可传异教，
chapters.en: As I besought thee to abide still at Ephesus, when I went into Macedonia, that thou mightest charge some that they teach no other doctrine,

chapters.zh: 也不可听从荒渺无凭的话语和无穷的家谱；这等事只生辩论，并不发明神在信上所立的章程。
chapters.en: Neither give heed to fables and endless genealogies, which minister questions, rather than godly edifying which is in faith: .

chapters.zh: 但命令的总归就是爱；这爱是从清洁的心和无亏的良心，无伪的信心生出来的。
chapters.en: Now the end of the commandment is charity out of a pure heart, and a good conscience, and faith unfeigned:

chapters.zh: 有人偏离这些，反去讲虚浮的话，
chapters.en: From which some having swerved have turned aside unto vain jangling;

chapters.zh: 想要作教法师，却不明白自己所讲说的所论定的。
chapters.en: Desiring to be teachers of the law; understanding neither what they say, nor whereof they affirm.

chapters.zh: 我们知道律法原是好的，只要人用得合宜；
chapters.en: But we know that the law good, if a man use it lawfully;

chapters.zh: 因为律法不是为义人设立的，乃是为不法和不服的，不虔诚和犯罪的，不圣洁和恋世俗的，弑父母和杀人的，
chapters.en: Knowing this, that the law is not made for a righteous man, but for the lawless and disobedient, for the ungodly and for sinners, for unholy and profane, for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers, for manslayers,

chapters.zh: 行淫和亲男色的，抢人口和说谎话的，并起假誓的，或是为别样敌正道的事设立的。
chapters.en: For whoremongers, for them that defile themselves with mankind, for menstealers, for liars, for perjured persons, and if there be any other thing that is contrary to sound doctrine;

chapters.zh: 这是照著可称颂之神交托我荣耀福音说的。
chapters.en: According to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.

chapters.zh: 我感谢那给我力量的我们主基督耶稣，因他以我有忠心，派我服事他。
chapters.en: And I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who hath enabled me, for that he counted me faithful, putting me into the ministry;

chapters.zh: 我从前是亵渎神的，逼迫人的，侮慢人的；然而我还蒙了怜悯，因我是不信不明白的时候而做的。
chapters.en: Who was before a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious: but I obtained mercy, because I did ignorantly in unbelief.

chapters.zh: 并且我主的恩是格外丰盛，使我在基督耶稣里有信心和爱心。
chapters.en: And the grace of our Lord was exceeding abundant with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.

chapters.zh: 基督耶稣降世，为要拯救罪人。这话是可信的，是十分可佩服的。在罪人中我是个罪魁。
chapters.en: This a faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief.

chapters.zh: 然而，我蒙了怜悯，是因耶稣基督要在我这罪魁身上显明他一切的忍耐，给后来信他得永生的人作榜样。
chapters.en: Howbeit for this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first Jesus Christ might shew forth all longsuffering, for a pattern to them which should hereafter believe on him to life everlasting.

chapters.zh: 但愿尊贵、荣耀归与那不能朽坏、不能看见、永世的君王、独一的神，直到永永远远。阿们！
chapters.en: Now unto the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only wise God, honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen.

chapters.zh: 我儿提摩太阿，我照从前指著你的预言，将这命令交托你，叫你因此可以打那美好的仗。
chapters.en: This charge I commit unto thee, son Timothy, according to the prophecies which went before on thee, that thou by them mightest war a good warfare;

chapters.zh: 常存信心和无亏的良心。有人丢弃良心，就在真道上如同船破坏了一般。
chapters.en: Holding faith, and a good conscience; which some having put away concerning faith have made shipwreck:

chapters.zh: 其中有许米乃和亚力山大；我已经把他们交给撒但，使他们受责罚就不再谤渎了。
chapters.en: Of whom is Hymenaeus and Alexander; whom I have delivered unto Satan, that they may learn not to blaspheme.

chapters.zh: 我劝你，第一要为万人恳求、祷告、代求、祝谢；
chapters.en: I exhort therefore, that, first of all, supplications, prayers, intercessions, giving of thanks, be made for all men;

chapters.zh: 为君王和一切在位的，也该如此，使我们可以敬虔、端正、平安无事的度日。
chapters.en: For kings, and all that are in authority; that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and honesty.

chapters.zh: 这是好的，在神我们救主面前可蒙悦纳。
chapters.en: For this good and acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour;

chapters.zh: 他愿意万人得救，明白真道。
chapters.en: Who will have all men to be saved, and to come unto the knowledge of the truth.

chapters.zh: 因为只有一位神，在神和人中间，只有一位中保，乃是降世为人的基督耶稣；
chapters.en: For one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus;

chapters.zh: 他舍自己作万人的赎价，到了时候，这事必证明出来。
chapters.en: Who gave himself a ransom for all, to be testified in due time.

chapters.zh: 我为此奉派作传道的，作使徒，作外邦人的师傅，教导他们相信，学习真道。我说的是真话，并不是谎言。
chapters.en: Whereunto I am ordained a preacher, and an apostle, (I speak the truth in Christ, lie not;) a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and verity.

chapters.zh: 我愿男人无忿怒，无争论（或作：疑惑），举起圣洁的手，随处祷告。
chapters.en: I will therefore that men pray every where, lifting up holy hands, without wrath and doubting.

chapters.zh: 又愿女人廉耻、自守，以正派衣裳为妆饰，不以编发、黄金、珍珠，和贵价的衣裳为妆饰；
chapters.en: In like manner also, that women adorn themselves in modest apparel, with shamefacedness and sobriety; not with broided hair, or gold, or pearls, or costly array;

chapters.zh: 只要有善行，这才与自称是敬神的女人相宜。
chapters.en: But (which becometh women professing godliness) with good works.

chapters.zh: 女人要沉静学道，一味的顺服。
chapters.en: Let the woman learn in silence with all subjection.

chapters.zh: 我不许女人讲道，也不许他辖管男人，只要沉静。
chapters.en: But I suffer not a woman to teach, nor to usurp authority over the man, but to be in silence.

chapters.zh: 因为先造的是亚当，后造的是夏娃；
chapters.en: For Adam was first formed, then Eve.

chapters.zh: 且不是亚当被引诱，乃是女人被引诱，陷在罪里。
chapters.en: And Adam was not deceived, but the woman being deceived was in the transgression.

chapters.zh: 然而，女人若常存信心、爱心，又圣洁自守，就必在生产上得救。
chapters.en: Notwithstanding she shall be saved in childbearing, if they continue in faith and charity and holiness with sobriety.

chapters.zh: 人若想要得监督的职分，就是羡慕善工。这话是可信的。
chapters.en: This a true saying, If a man desire the office of a bishop, he desireth a good work.

chapters.zh: 作监督的，必须无可指责，只作一个妇人的丈夫，有节制，自守，端正，乐意接待远人，善于教导；
chapters.en: A bishop then must be blameless, the husband of one wife, vigilant, sober, of good behaviour, given to hospitality, apt to teach;

chapters.zh: 不因酒滋事，不打人，只要温和，不争竞，不贪财；
chapters.en: Not given to wine, no striker, not greedy of filthy lucre; but patient, not a brawler, not covetous;

chapters.zh: 好好管理自己的家，使儿女凡事端庄顺服（或作：端端庄庄地使儿女顺服）。
chapters.en: One that ruleth well his own house, having his children in subjection with all gravity;

chapters.zh: 人若不知道管理自己的家，焉能照管神的教会呢？
chapters.en: (For if a man know not how to rule his own house, how shall he take care of the church of God?)

chapters.zh: 初入教的不可作监督，恐怕他自高自大，就落在魔鬼所受的刑罚里。
chapters.en: Not a novice, lest being lifted up with pride he fall into the condemnation of the devil.

chapters.zh: 监督也必须在教外有好名声，恐怕被人毁谤，落在魔鬼的网罗里。
chapters.en: Moreover he must have a good report of them which are without; lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.

chapters.zh: 作执事的，也是如此：必须端庄，不一口两舌，不好喝酒，不贪不义之财；
chapters.en: Likewise the deacons grave, not doubletongued, not given to much wine, not greedy of filthy lucre;

chapters.zh: 要存清洁的良心，固守真道的奥秘。
chapters.en: Holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.

chapters.zh: 这等人也要先受试验，若没有可责之处，然后叫他们作执事。
chapters.en: And let these also first be proved; then let them use the office of a deacon, being blameless.

chapters.zh: 女执事（原文是女人）也是如此：必须端庄，不说谗言，有节制，凡事忠心。
chapters.en: Even so wives grave, not slanderers, sober, faithful in all things.

chapters.zh: 执事只要作一个妇人的丈夫，好好管理儿女和自己的家。
chapters.en: Let the deacons be the husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.

chapters.zh: 因为善作执事的，自己就得到美好的地步，并且在基督耶稣里的真道上大有胆量。
chapters.en: For they that have used the office of a deacon well purchase to themselves a good degree, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.

chapters.zh: 我指望快到你那里去，所以先将这些事写给你。
chapters.en: These things write I unto thee, hoping to come unto thee shortly:

chapters.zh: 倘若我耽延日久，你也可以知道在神的家中当怎样行。这家就是永生神的教会，真理的柱石和根基。
chapters.en: But if I tarry long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.

chapters.zh: 大哉，敬虔的奥秘！无人不以为然：就是神在肉身显现，被圣灵称义（或作：在灵性称义），被天使看见，被传于外邦，被世人信服，被接在荣耀里。
chapters.en: And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifest in the flesh, justified in the Spirit, seen of angels, preached unto the Gentiles, believed on in the world, received up into glory.

chapters.zh: 圣灵明说，在后来的时候，必有人离弃真道，听从那引诱人的邪灵和鬼魔的道理。
chapters.en: Now the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils;

chapters.zh: 这是因为说谎之人的假冒；这等人的良心如同被热铁烙惯了一般。
chapters.en: Speaking lies in hypocrisy; having their conscience seared with a hot iron;

chapters.zh: 他们禁止嫁娶，又禁戒食物（或作：又叫人戒荤），就是神所造、叫那信而明白真道的人感谢著领受的。
chapters.en: Forbidding to marry, to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving of them which believe and know the truth.

chapters.zh: 凡神所造的物都是好的，若感谢著领受，就没有一样可弃的，
chapters.en: For every creature of God good, and nothing to be refused, if it be received with thanksgiving:

chapters.zh: 都因神的道和人的祈求成为圣洁了。
chapters.en: For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.

chapters.zh: 你若将这些事提醒弟兄们，便是基督耶稣的好执事，在真道的话语和你向来所服从的善道上得了教育。
chapters.en: If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things, thou shalt be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished up in the words of faith and of good doctrine, whereunto thou hast attained.

chapters.zh: 只是要弃绝那世俗的言语和老妇荒渺的话，在敬虔上操练自己。
chapters.en: But refuse profane and old wives' fables, and exercise thyself unto godliness.

chapters.zh: 操练身体，益处还少；惟独敬虔，凡事都有益处，因有今生和来生的应许。
chapters.en: For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.

chapters.zh: 这话是可信的，是十分可佩服的。
chapters.en: This a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation.

chapters.zh: 我们劳苦努力，正是为此，因我们的指望在乎永生的神；他是万人的救主，更是信徒的救主。
chapters.en: For therefore we both labour and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, specially of those that believe.

chapters.zh: 这些事，你要吩咐人，也要教导人。
chapters.en: These things command and teach.

chapters.zh: 不可叫人小看你年轻，总要在言语、行为、爱心、信心、清洁上，都作信徒的榜样。
chapters.en: Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity.

chapters.zh: 你要以宣读、劝勉、教导为念，直等到我来。
chapters.en: Till I come, give attendance to reading, to exhortation, to doctrine.

chapters.zh: 你不要轻忽所得的恩赐，就是从前藉著预言、在众长老按手的时候赐给你的。
chapters.en: Neglect not the gift that is in thee, which was given thee by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the presbytery.

chapters.zh: 这些事你要殷勤去做，并要在此专心，使众人看出你的长进来。
chapters.en: Meditate upon these things; give thyself wholly to them; that thy profiting may appear to all.

chapters.zh: 你要谨慎自己和自己的教训，要在这些事上恒心；因为这样行，又能救自己，又能救听你的人。
chapters.en: Take heed unto thyself, and unto the doctrine; continue in them: for in doing this thou shalt both save thyself, and them that hear thee.

chapters.zh: 不可严责老年人，只要劝他如同父亲；劝少年人如同弟兄；
chapters.en: Rebuke not an elder, but intreat as a father; the younger men as brethren;

chapters.zh: 劝老年妇女如同母亲；劝少年妇女如同姐妹；总要清清洁洁的。
chapters.en: The elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, with all purity.

chapters.zh: 要尊敬那真为寡妇的。
chapters.en: Honour widows that are widows indeed.

chapters.zh: 若寡妇有儿女，或有孙子孙女，便叫他们先在自己家中学著行孝，报答亲恩，因为这在神面前是可悦纳的。
chapters.en: But if any widow have children or nephews, let them learn first to shew piety at home, and to requite their parents: for that is good and acceptable before God.

chapters.zh: 那独居无靠、真为寡妇的，是仰赖神，昼夜不住的祈求祷告。
chapters.en: Now she that is a widow indeed, and desolate, trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day.

chapters.zh: 但那好宴乐的寡妇正活著的时候也是死的。
chapters.en: But she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth.

chapters.zh: 这些事你要嘱咐他们，叫他们无可指责。
chapters.en: And these things give in charge, that they may be blameless.

chapters.zh: 人若不看顾亲属，就是背了真道，比不信的人还不好，不看顾自己家里的人，更是如此。
chapters.en: But if any provide not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel.

chapters.zh: 寡妇记在册子上，必须年纪到六十岁，从来只作一个丈夫的妻子，
chapters.en: Let not a widow be taken into the number under threescore years old, having been the wife of one man,

chapters.zh: 又有行善的名声，就如养育儿女，接待远人，洗圣徒的脚，救济遭难的人，竭力行各样善事。
chapters.en: Well reported of for good works; if she have brought up children, if she have lodged strangers, if she have washed the saints' feet, if she have relieved the afflicted, if she have diligently followed every good work.

chapters.zh: 至于年轻的寡妇，就可以辞他；因为他们的情欲发动，违背基督的时候就想要嫁人。
chapters.en: But the younger widows refuse: for when they have begun to wax wanton against Christ, they will marry;

chapters.zh: 他们被定罪，是因废弃了当初所许的愿；
chapters.en: Having damnation, because they have cast off their first faith.

chapters.zh: 并且他们又习惯懒惰，挨家闲游；不但是懒惰，又说长道短，好管闲事，说些不当说的话。
chapters.en: And withal they learn idle, wandering about from house to house; and not only idle, but tattlers also and busybodies, speaking things which they ought not.

chapters.zh: 所以我愿意年轻的寡妇嫁人，生养儿女，治理家务，不给敌人辱骂的把柄。
chapters.en: I will therefore that the younger women marry, bear children, guide the house, give none occasion to the adversary to speak reproachfully.

chapters.zh: 因为已经有转去随从撒但的。
chapters.en: For some are already turned aside after Satan.

chapters.zh: 信主的妇女，若家中有寡妇，自己就当救济他们，不可累著教会，好使教会能救济那真无倚靠的寡妇。
chapters.en: If any man or woman that believeth have widows, let them relieve them, and let not the church be charged; that it may relieve them that are widows indeed.

chapters.zh: 那善于管理教会的长老，当以为配受加倍的敬奉；那劳苦传道教导人的，更当如此。
chapters.en: Let the elders that rule well be counted worthy of double honour, especially they who labour in the word and doctrine.

chapters.zh: 因为经上说：牛在场上踹谷的时候，不可笼住他的嘴；又说：工人得工价是应当的。
chapters.en: For the scripture saith, Thou shalt not muzzle the ox that treadeth out the corn. And, The labourer worthy of his reward.

chapters.zh: 控告长老的呈子，非有两三个见证就不要收。
chapters.en: Against an elder receive not an accusation, but before two or three witnesses.

chapters.zh: 犯罪的人，当在众人面前责备他，叫其余的人也可以惧怕。
chapters.en: Them that sin rebuke before all, that others also may fear.

chapters.zh: 我在神和基督耶稣并蒙拣选的天使面前嘱咐你：要遵守这些话，不可存成见，行事也不可有偏心。
chapters.en: I charge before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the elect angels, that thou observe these things without preferring one before another, doing nothing by partiality.

chapters.zh: 给人行按手的礼，不可急促；不要在别人的罪上有分，要保守自己清洁。
chapters.en: Lay hands suddenly on no man, neither be partaker of other men's sins: keep thyself pure.

chapters.zh: 因你胃口不清，屡次患病，再不要照常喝水，可以稍微用点酒。
chapters.en: Drink no longer water, but use a little wine for thy stomach's sake and thine often infirmities.

chapters.zh: 有些人的罪是明显的，如同先到审判案前；有些人的罪是随后跟了去的。
chapters.en: Some men's sins are open beforehand, going before to judgment; and some they follow after.

chapters.zh: 这样，善行也有明显的，那不明显的也不能隐藏。
chapters.en: Likewise also the good works are manifest beforehand; and they that are otherwise cannot be hid.

chapters.zh: 凡在轭下作仆人的，当以自己主人配受十分的恭敬，免得神的名和道理被人亵渎。
chapters.en: Let as many servants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honour, that the name of God and doctrine be not blasphemed.

chapters.zh: 仆人有信道的主人，不可因为与他是弟兄就轻看他；更要加意服事他；因为得服事之益处的，是信道蒙爱的。你要以此教训人，劝勉人。
chapters.en: And they that have believing masters, let them not despise , because they are brethren; but rather do service, because they are faithful and beloved, partakers of the benefit. These things teach and exhort.

chapters.zh: 若有人传异教，不服从我们主耶稣基督纯正的话与那合乎敬虔的道理，
chapters.en: If any man teach otherwise, and consent not to wholesome words, the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness;

chapters.zh: 他是自高自大，一无所知，专好问难，争辩言词，从此就生出嫉妒、分争、毁谤、妄疑，
chapters.en: He is proud, knowing nothing, but doting about questions and strifes of words, whereof cometh envy, strife, railings, evil surmisings,

chapters.zh: 并那坏了心术、失丧真理之人的争竞。他们以敬虔为得利的门路。
chapters.en: Perverse disputings of men of corrupt minds, and destitute of the truth, supposing that gain is godliness: from such withdraw thyself.

chapters.zh: 然而，敬虔加上知足的心便是大利了；
chapters.en: But godliness with contentment is great gain.

chapters.zh: 因为我们没有带甚么到世上来，也不能带甚么去。
chapters.en: For we brought nothing into world, certain we can carry nothing out.

chapters.zh: 只要有衣有食，就当知足。
chapters.en: And having food and raiment let us be therewith content.

chapters.zh: 但那些想要发财的人，就陷在迷惑、落在网罗和许多无知有害的私欲里，叫人沉在败坏和灭亡中。
chapters.en: But they that will be rich fall into temptation and a snare, and many foolish and hurtful lusts, which drown men in destruction and perdition.

chapters.zh: 贪财是万恶之根。有人贪恋钱财，就被引诱离了真道，用许多愁苦把自己刺透了。
chapters.en: For the love of money is the root of all evil: which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith, and pierced themselves through with many sorrows.

chapters.zh: 但你这属神的人要逃避这些事，追求公义、敬虔、信心、爱心、忍耐、温柔。
chapters.en: But thou, O man of God, flee these things; and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, meekness.

chapters.zh: 你要为真道打那美好的仗，持定永生。你为此被召，也在许多见证人面前，已经作了那美好的见证。
chapters.en: Fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on eternal life, whereunto thou art also called, and hast professed a good profession before many witnesses.

chapters.zh: 我在叫万物生活的神面前，并在向本丢彼拉多作过那美好见证的基督耶稣面前嘱咐你：
chapters.en: I give thee charge in the sight of God, who quickeneth all things, and Christ Jesus, who before Pontius Pilate witnessed a good confession;

chapters.zh: 要守这命令，毫不玷污，无可指责，直到我们的主耶稣基督显现。
chapters.en: That thou keep commandment without spot, unrebukeable, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ:

chapters.zh: 到了日期，那可称颂、独有权能的万王之王、万主之主，
chapters.en: Which in his times he shall shew, the blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings, and Lord of lords;

chapters.zh: 就是那独一不死、住在人不能靠近的光里，是人未曾看见、也是不能看见的，要将他显明出来。但愿尊贵和永远的权能都归给他。阿们！
chapters.en: Who only hath immortality, dwelling in the light which no man can approach unto; whom no man hath seen, nor can see: to whom honour and power everlasting. Amen.

chapters.zh: 你要嘱咐那些今世富足的人，不要自高，也不要倚靠无定的钱财；只要倚靠那厚赐百物给我们享受的神。
chapters.en: Charge them that are rich in this world, that they be not highminded, nor trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God, who giveth us richly all things to enjoy;

chapters.zh: 又要嘱咐他们行善，在好事上富足，甘心施舍，乐意供给（或作：体贴）人，
chapters.en: That they do good, that they be rich in good works, ready to distribute, willing to communicate;

chapters.zh: 为自己积成美好的根基，预备将来，叫他们持定那真正的生命。
chapters.en: Laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold on eternal life.

chapters.zh: 提摩太阿，你要保守所托付你的，躲避世俗的虚谈和那敌真道、似是而非的学问。
chapters.en: O Timothy, keep that which is committed to thy trust, avoiding profane vain babblings, and oppositions of science falsely so called:

chapters.zh: 已经有人自称有这学问，就偏离了真道。愿恩惠常与你们同在！
chapters.en: Which some professing have erred concerning the faith. Grace with thee. Amen. « »
